title: WhiskyDB — Fine Spirits & Whisky Sample Dataset
subtitle: Whiskies, distilleries and open cask & flavor taxonomies — provenance-tracked
description:
# 🥃 WhiskyDB — Fine Spirits & Whisky Sample Dataset

A curated **free sample** of the WhiskyDB dataset: whiskies & fine spirits built **entirely from open, legally accessible public sources** — government label registries (US TTB COLA), corporate registries (UK Companies House), the EU eAmbrosia GI register, Open Food Facts, Wikipedia/Wikidata, and open auction statistics — with a **provenance ledger entry behind every record**.

## What's in this sample

| File | Rows | What it shows |
| :--- | ---: | :--- |
| `spirits.csv` | 15 | Bottlings: name, type, age, ABV, volume + provenance |
| `distilleries.csv` | 15 | Producers: name, country, region + provenance |
| `casks.csv` | 13 | **Full open taxonomy** — 3-tier cask hierarchy (CC-BY-4.0) |
| `flavors.csv` | 15 | **Full open taxonomy** — controlled flavor vocabulary (CC-BY-4.0) |

The two taxonomy files are the **complete open-source taxonomies** (CC-BY-4.0 — free for any use incl. commercial, with attribution). The two sample files are curated subsets of the full commercial dataset.

## The full dataset

- **1,290+** spirits & bottlings · **3,200+** distilleries, brands & producers · **110+** countries
- **20,000+ monthly auction-price benchmarks** (Nov 2005 → today) — distillery-level market indices, honestly labeled, ideal for valuation & trend models
- **270+** protected GI appellations (EU/UK)
- 10-table relational SQLite + CSV, 100% provenance-tracked (every row resolves to a source ledger entry)
- Refreshed **monthly** from the US TTB COLA label registry

**→ Get it at [whisky-database.pages.dev](https://whisky-database.pages.dev/)** · [GitHub: WhiskyyDB/whisky-database](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database) · [Data dictionary](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database/blob/main/DATA_DICTIONARY.md) · [Sources & licenses](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database/blob/main/SOURCES.md)

## Honest coverage notes

Public sources don't all publish every attribute: `age` is sparse (most listings are NAS), and `abv` is explicitly sourced for 16% of full-dataset records (e.g. federal label details) with a documented 40.0 default elsewhere. Field-level coverage numbers are published up front in the [README](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database#field-coverage-the-honest-numbers).

## Provenance & collection methodology

Every record traces to a `data_sources` provenance ledger entry (source name, endpoint, license, access timestamp) — the `source_name` / `source_url` columns on each sample row are that ledger surfaced. How the data is collected:

1. **Government label registries** — US TTB COLA label approvals (public domain, 17 U.S.C. § 105) queried via the COLA Cloud API: brand names, spirit classes, label ABVs, barcodes. Refreshed monthly by a scheduled pipeline that deduplicates by canonical spirit name before ingesting.
2. **Corporate registries** — UK Companies House API (OGL v3.0): distillery incorporations, founding years, jurisdiction derived from company-number prefixes.
3. **GI registers** — EU eAmbrosia (EU open data): 270+ protected spirit appellations (PGI/PDO).
4. **Open product databases** — Open Food Facts bulk dump (ODbL), filtered through a spirit-plausibility gate (name patterns + 30–70% ABV band) to exclude non-spirit products, and a Latin-script filter for name quality.
5. **Open encyclopedias** — Wikipedia distillery/brand lists (CC-BY-SA) and Wikidata SPARQL (CC0): names, regions, founding years.
6. **Open auction statistics** — WhiskyHunter API: distillery-level monthly auction indices, honestly labeled as market-level (never bottle-specific realizations).
7. **Curated seed** — 6 benchmark spirits hand-curated from producers' official publications (mash bills, cask programs, tasting descriptors), documented in [SOURCES.md](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database/blob/main/SOURCES.md#whiskydb-curated-seed).

All scraping is polite (rate-limited, identified user agent, public endpoints only). Records are normalized into a 10-table relational schema; no values are invented — attributes a source doesn't publish stay empty or carry a documented default (see coverage notes above). Full per-source licensing: [SOURCES.md](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database/blob/main/SOURCES.md).

## Use cases

- Bar-menu & hospitality software (clean names, types, ABV, volumes)
- Collection-tracking and spirits-discovery apps
- Whisky investment analytics on a 19-year monthly auction index (full dataset)
- Flavor/cask-based recommendation engines on the open taxonomies
- ML / RAG corpora over a provenance-tracked spirits knowledge base

## License

Sample CSVs: **CC-BY-NC-4.0**. Taxonomies (`casks.csv`, `flavors.csv`): **CC-BY-4.0**. Records derived from ODbL/CC-BY-SA sources retain upstream attribution obligations — see [SOURCES.md](https://github.com/WhiskyyDB/whisky-database/blob/main/SOURCES.md).

Questions, corrections, or full-dataset access: **whiskeydn.kite979@simplelogin.com**

resources.description: Curated sample of 15 whisky/spirit bottlings from the full 1,290+ record dataset, with provenance columns.

fields.name: spirit_id
fields.description: Stable integer primary key.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Canonical bottling name (Latin-script, cleaned).

fields.name: type
fields.description: Spirit classification: Single Malt Scotch, Bourbon, Rye Whiskey, Irish Whiskey, Japanese Whisky, Scotch Whisky, Whisky, etc.

fields.name: age
fields.description: Age statement in years; empty = NAS (no age statement) or unstated.

fields.name: abv
fields.description: Alcohol by volume (%). Explicitly sourced where available (e.g. US federal label details); otherwise the documented 40.0 default.

fields.name: volume_ml
fields.description: Bottle volume normalized to milliliters (700/750/1000...).

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Provenance: name of the open source the record came from.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Provenance: source endpoint URL.

resources.description: Curated sample of 15 distilleries/producers from the full 3,200+ record dataset, with provenance columns.

fields.name: distillery_id
fields.description: Stable integer primary key.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Distillery / producer / brand name.

fields.name: country
fields.description: Country, normalized (USA, Scotland, Northern Ireland...); 'Global' when the source doesn't state one.

fields.name: region
fields.description: Region / locality (Islay, Kentucky, GI category...).

fields.name: source_name
fields.description: Provenance: name of the open source the record came from.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: Provenance: source endpoint URL.

resources.description: Complete open-source 3-tier cask maturation taxonomy (CC-BY-4.0): category -> sub-type -> specific style, with wood species.

fields.name: category
fields.description: Tier 1 cask category: Sherry, Bourbon, Port, Wine, Rum, Virgin Oak, Japanese Oak.

fields.name: sub_type
fields.description: Tier 2 sub-type: Pedro Ximenez, Oloroso, Ex-Bourbon, Ruby Port, Mizunara...

fields.name: specific_style
fields.description: Tier 3 specific style: PX Sherry Butt, 1st Fill Bourbon Barrel, Mizunara Oak Cask...

fields.name: wood_species
fields.description: Botanical oak species: Quercus alba, Quercus robur, Quercus mongolica.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Short human-readable description of the cask style.

resources.description: Complete open-source controlled flavor vocabulary (CC-BY-4.0) for standardized whisky tasting descriptors.

fields.name: macro_category
fields.description: Flavor macro-category: Peat & Smoke, Sweet & Vanilla, Fruity, Spicy & Woody, Rich & Nutty.

fields.name: descriptor
fields.description: Controlled flavor descriptor: Peat Smoke, Vanilla, Dried Figs, Oak Spice...
